docs.title: #search-team thread
docs.content:
Thread in #search-team:
sam: Is there a doc on the embedding pipeline? I need to understand how we handle long chunks before the vector store.
tina: There's a Notion page on the retrieval architecture - it covers chunk sizing and the 8192 token limit.
sam: Ah got it, I'll check that.

docs.title: Retrieval Architecture
docs.content:
Retrieval Architecture

## Embedding pipeline
Documents are chunked to ~512 tokens (target) with 50-token overlap. Each chunk is embedded using text-embedding-3-large (max 8192 tokens). Chunks exceeding 8192 tokens are truncated with a warning logged.

## Vector store
Embeddings are stored in Neon Postgres using pgvector with an HNSW index (m=16, ef_construction=64).

## Retrieval
At query time, the query is embedded and a cosine ANN search returns the top 50 candidates, which are then fused with BM25 via RRF.

expected_edges.note: Slack thread mentions the Notion retrieval architecture page in passing as a pointer, without analyzing or discussing its content
